economic_claim_note: Each listed Class A share represents one OpCo unit. Valuation therefore uses 27.839 million Class A plus 49.178 million exchangeable Class B/OpCo units, rather than dividing consolidated cash flow by Class A shares alone. Sponsor control, tax distributions, and related-party WaterBridge transactions remain governance risks.
pricing_conclusion: LandBridge has scarce Delaware Basin acreage and a capital-light fee model. Actual purchases validate roughly $0.9k-$7.1k per acre and approximately 7.6x EBITDA for cash-flowing packages, while its current fee engine already receives a premium 15x base multiple. After improving the dormant-acreage and powered-land option marks, $78.12 remains well above base value but is close to the high economic case. The result is a watch, with the market already paying for successful digital-infrastructure conversion and continued acquisition accretion.
strongest_counter_explanation: LandBridge may be evolving from an oilfield surface landlord into a uniquely advantaged power, water, pore-space, and digital-infrastructure platform whose recurring economics deserve a multiple above the base case.
falsifiers: Alpha Digital discloses contracted rent or royalty economics that independently add more than $8 per economic unit.
Arm's-length revenue excluding WaterBridge compounds above 12% while net leverage declines.
Acquisitions continue below 8x forward EBITDA without equity dilution or related-party leakage.

economic_value_bridge.gaap_role: Reference only. Acquisition accounting records recent land closer to transaction value, but legacy acreage, pore space, water, and assembled contiguity are not represented by consolidated book value.
economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: The 2025 10-K allocated recent purchases among land, surface-use agreements, and brackish-water assets; these allocations are cross-checks, not the valuation conclusion.

component_groups.label: Current surface, resource, and royalty fee engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: $180M/$220M/$240M EBITDA at 10x/15x/20x; the base is materially above the approximately 7.6x multiple paid for LandBridge's cash-flowing Q4 2024 acreage packages.
component_groups.overlap_control: Currently monetized acreage, water royalties, and surface-use contracts are embedded here and not added as full land NAV.

component_groups.label: Net debt and financing claims
component_groups.valuation_basis: Balance-sheet claim across all 77.017M economic units.

component_groups.label: Dormant and under-monetized acreage
component_groups.valuation_basis: Only an illustrative 20% of 320,000 acres receives a separate risked mark against actual $0.9k-$7.1k per-acre purchases.
component_groups.overlap_control: The remaining acreage is embedded in the fee engine.

component_groups.label: Alpha Digital powered-land option
component_groups.valuation_basis: Milestone option on up to 3,400 acres and 2 GW; powered-land scarcity raises success value, but undisclosed rent and customer terms keep the probability-weighted base modest.
component_groups.overlap_control: No full campus value is capitalized before customer, power, and lease economics are contractual.

component_groups.label: Pore space, water, fiber, and other new uses
component_groups.valuation_basis: Portfolio option reserve informed by the 1918 Ranch allocation and Intrepid South Ranch water/land transaction.
component_groups.overlap_control: Existing WaterBridge-related cash flow stays in the fee engine.

wisdom_applied: Horizon Kinetics: prioritize the 80% free-cash-flow-margin royalty and toll structure over GAAP land carrying value.
Groundbreaker: contiguous powered land and local water are scarce physical bottlenecks, but first-order power enthusiasm may already be priced.
Klarman: transaction-backed land value and uncontracted catalysts are shown separately.
Marks: sponsor control, related-party revenue, and leverage remain claims against realization value.
limitations: LandBridge does not disclose Alpha Digital rent, customer commitments, or full powered-land economics.
Per-acre purchases include different mixes of fee title, leasehold, water, pore space, and existing contracts and cannot be applied uniformly.
The 20% dormant-acreage split is provisional pending parcel-level revenue attribution.

inputs.shares_source: Q1 2026 10-Q: 27,839,229 Class A plus 49,177,775 Class B/OpCo units
inputs.fcf_source: Normalized consolidated owner cash of approximately $139M divided by 77.017M economic units; bracketed by FY2025 FCF of $120M and Q1 2026 annualized FCF of $164M.

bear.notes: Permian slowdown, related-party pressure, and no digital conversion.

base.notes: Capital-light surface cash grows with the 2026 guide; options remain outside owner cash.

bull.notes: Water, pore space, and digital leases convert while leverage declines.

components.label: Current surface, resource, and royalty fee engine

valuation.evidence: 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance is $210M-$230M; values use $180M at 10x, $220M at 15x, and $240M at 20x.
valuation.cross_check: Q1 2026 revenue $51.0M, EBITDA $44.9M, FCF $40.9M; do not separately add currently monetized acreage.
valuation.assumption_summary: Enterprise value before net debt; the base multiple is deliberately generous for scarce, capital-light acreage.

valuation.evidence: Q1 2026 long-term debt was approximately $535M; year-end cash was $30.7M.
valuation.cross_check: Base approximates $505M net debt divided by 77.017M economic units.

valuation.evidence: LandBridge purchases ranged from approximately $875 per acre for an 800-acre bolt-on to roughly $7,100 per fee acre in the 1918 Ranch allocation. Only an illustrative 20% of 320k acres is separately marked after overlap and realization discounts.
valuation.cross_check: The remaining acreage and all current revenue are embedded in the fee-engine valuation.

valuation.evidence: PowerBridge holds an option over up to 3,400 acres; first power is targeted for 2027 and scaled generation for 2028. Powered land is scarce, but customer, rent, capital, and enforceable full-campus economics remain undisclosed.
valuation.cross_check: The base remains below $250M across all economic units; no success-case campus value is treated as current contracted rent.

components.label: Pore space and other new-use options

valuation.evidence: Contiguous acreage may support pore space, power, fiber, water, and other industrial uses. The 1918 Ranch and Intrepid South Ranch transactions establish that these rights have non-zero value even before full monetization.
valuation.cross_check: Options graduate into current operations only after contracts establish economics.
